policy_statement: Starting in 2020, DPD staff will assess Class I landfill biogas capture performance versus the 75% national average and, if feasible, launch an implementation and monitoring plan to achieve an 85% landfill biogas capture rate by 2020.
mechanism_description: DPD staff plan to assess landfill biogas capture performance and, if feasible, implement and monitor improvements to increase capture, reducing methane emissions from the landfill.
primary_causal_pathway: Waste disposal (landfills) → ↑ landfill gas capture/oxidation or destruction → ↓ fugitive CH₄ emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail:
(1) Change in activity/system: Landfill operations are modified to install/upgrade gas collection wells, piping, covers, dewatering, and control systems, and to route collected gas to destruction (flare/engine) or beneficial use rather than venting/leaking.  
(2) Mediating process: Methanogenic decomposition in landfills produces CH₄; improved collection reduces fugitive pathways, and combustion converts CH₄ to CO₂ (lower GWP) and water, while beneficial use can also displace some fossil energy.  
(3) Climate-relevant outcome: Lower net CH₄ emissions from landfills (and sometimes additional CO₂ displacement), yielding reduced near-term and long-term radiative forcing.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reducing net greenhouse gas emissions by capturing and destroying/using landfill methane that would otherwise leak to the atmosphere.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Improved landfill gas capture and destruction reduces fugitive methane, producing an absolute GHG emissions decrease.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While energy recovery can improve resource efficiency or displace fossil generation, the largest and most direct climate effect across this mechanism is methane avoidance/destruction (mitigation).  
3) Edge cases applied: If a policy focuses only on energy generation from already-captured gas, it could tilt toward resource efficiency; however, this canonical mechanism explicitly includes capture/control upgrades and methane emissions reduction, making mitigation dominant.  
4) Confirm applies to ALL instances: Any policy whose core mechanism is capturing and destroying landfill gas to reduce methane emissions follows the same mitigation pathway regardless of location, landfill class, or technology choice (flare vs engine).  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: Whether benefits are primarily from flaring vs electricity generation, and the magnitude of any fossil displacement co-benefit, must be assessed at the individual policy level.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If a policy frames the action purely as renewable energy production from landfill gas (with capture assumed baseline), classification could shift toward Resource Efficiency; but when capture/control expansion is integral (as here), Mitigation remains the appropriate primary category.
key_indicators: "biogas capture rate compares with the national average rate of 75%"; "implementation and monitoring plan"; "achieve a landfill biogas capture rate of 85% by 2020"; "assess ... performance"
co_benefits: Potential local air quality/odor reductions and improved landfill safety/operations (not explicitly stated).
instance_edge_case_notes: No explicit regulatory mandate or funding mechanism is described; the commitment is framed as inquiry/coordination and launching a plan conditional on feasibility, so it is primarily a target-setting/planning commitment rather than an enforceable performance standard.
